rules.text: Ensure numpy is imported as np.
Use only markdown headers of level ### or lower for all document structure.
Always adhere to mandatory heading naming, required checklist inclusion, and explicit dirty bit validation.
Always include the required structural headers before developing content to ensure strict compliance.
Maintain a flat hierarchy, exclude code, and define validation logic explicitly in all sections.
Enforce modular data structures, plain-text headers, and explicit state-validation logic in all future drafts.
Remove irrelevant libraries, mandate learner synthesis tasks, and define state-locking mechanisms for policy validation.
Use only four headers, exclude all code, and strictly align content with core constraints.
Draft technical documentation using four headers, structural data formats, and mandatory verification checklists only.
Restrict content strictly to defined curriculum components; exclude extraneous technical concepts and include mandatory elements.
Strictly align verification requirements with documented curriculum scope, avoiding hallucinations and internal logic contradictions.
Strictly adhere to the mandatory header structure and sequence in all initial draft submissions.
Mandatory inclusion of the ### Verification Test heading must precede all architecture documentation.
Remove headers, include mandatory verification gates, and implement functional code for all structural requirements.
Adhere strictly to authorized header hierarchies and mandated section ordering in all documentation drafts.
Prioritize robust input validation and state isolation before executing agent telemetry logic gates.
Replace all Markdown headers with bold text and implement programmatic logic for state verification.
Use only level 3 headers, follow the mandatory section order, and include no code.
Align technical terminology with the curriculum and eliminate extraneous, non-prescriptive architectural analogies.
Integrate functional code blocks and consolidated validation checklists into a strictly standardized document structure.
Adhere to strict flat headers, embed actionable checklists, and provide functional code implementation only.
Align technical complexity and performance metrics strictly with the defined pedagogical scope and functionality.
Integrate verification checklists into bulleted sections and strictly prohibit sub-headers and trailing markdown lists.
Align implementation details strictly with curriculum requirements, removing contradictory constraints and unsupported telemetry metrics.
Use only level 3 (###) headers; never use level 1 (#) or level 2 (##) headers.
Use only level three (###) or lower headers to ensure structural validation compliance.
Use exclusively level three (###) or lower headers to ensure structural validation.
Remove all markdown headers and include commented code blocks demonstrating tool utilization.
Use only level three or lower headers to ensure compliance with document structural standards.
Use only headers level ### or lower; never use level # or ## for content.
Include annotated code blocks to illustrate technical concepts while avoiding bold text within headers.
Use only level 3 (###) or lower headers for all documentation sections.
Use only level three (###) or lower headers to ensure structural validation compliance.
Ensure all examples strictly mirror the defined output format to prevent structural instructions conflicts.
Always include heavily commented code blocks to demonstrate all technical implementation requirements.
Use only level three (###) or lower headers; remove all single-level (#) documentation headers.
Standardize heading hierarchy, link all anchors, and ensure uniform footer formatting across all modules.
Use only level 3 headers (###) or lower; never use level 1 (#) headers.
Use only level 3 (###) headers; exclude markdown headers from code block comments.
Use only level three (###) or deeper headers for all structural formatting.
Use only triple-hash (###) or lower headers for all document section titles.
Distinguish goal initialization from the three-stage functional loop to ensure correct task-based termination logic.
Use only headers level 3 (###) or lower to maintain structural compliance during drafting.
